new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a wine of fresh grapes certifying that it carries a protected geographical indication (PGI) as defined in EU wine regulations, distinguishing it from wines with a protected designation of origin (PDO) or wines without such a geographical indication.

new_classes.label_en: still wine of fresh grapes with PGI in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2204 21 79–80)
new_classes.label_de: Wein aus frischen Weintrauben mit g.g.A. in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L (KN 2204 21 79–80)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected geographical indication (PGI), presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, as covered by CN codes 2204 21 79 and 2204 21 80. Distinguishable from PDO wines (CN 2204 21 11–78) and from wines in containers over 2 L (CN 2204 29).

new_classes.label_en: still wine of fresh grapes with PGI, in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2204 21 80)
new_classes.label_de: Wein aus frischen Weintrauben mit g.g.A., in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L (KN 2204 21 80)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected geographical indication (PGI), presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN code 2204 21 80. See additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22 for the definition of PGI wines.

coverage_explanation: The legal text specifies two key criteria: (1) the wine bears a protected geographical indication (PGI) — captured by the new ProtectedGeographicalIndicationQuality class and its restriction on StillWineWithPGI2204Small and the leaf class StillWinePGISmallContainer_22042180; (2) the wine is presented in containers ≤ 2 L — captured by the SmallContainer part restriction. The PGI is explicitly distinguished from PDO via the complement restriction. However, the legal text references additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22 for the precise definition of PGI wines, whose full content (e.g. specific alcoholic strength thresholds, sub-categorisations distinguishing 22042179 from 22042180) is not provided in the supplied notes, so the exact discriminating criterion between sibling codes 22042179 and 22042180 (likely an alcoholic strength sub-range) cannot be axiomatised, reducing coverage. The still (non-sparkling, non-semi-sparkling) nature of the wine is implied by the parent hierarchy (StillWineWithPGI2204Small / WineOfFreshGrapes) but not explicitly axiomatised via a carbonation quality restriction, which is a minor gap.
